coreConcept: The water cycle is how water moves around Earth in a repeating loop: it heats up, rises, cools, and falls. This cycle keeps water moving through the air, land, and oceans so plants, animals, and people have water to use.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: The Sun
keyActorsAndComponents.description: The Sun warms Earth and gives energy that turns liquid water into vapor.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Bodies of Water
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Oceans, lakes, and rivers hold most of Earth’s water that can evaporate into the air.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: The Atmosphere (Air)
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Air carries invisible water vapor and holds clouds that move water around the planet.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Clouds
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Clouds form when water vapor cools and sticks together into tiny droplets or ice crystals.

keyActorsAndComponents.name: Land and Plants
keyActorsAndComponents.description: Land stores water in soil and rivers; plants release water vapor through transpiration and help return water to the air.

storyOfProcess.name: Evaporation and Transpiration
storyOfProcess.description: Sun heats water in oceans, lakes, and soil and plants release vapor, turning liquid water into invisible water vapor that rises into the air.

storyOfProcess.name: Cooling and Condensation
storyOfProcess.description: As water vapor rises it cools and changes into tiny droplets or ice crystals, forming clouds.

storyOfProcess.name: Precipitation
storyOfProcess.description: When droplets in clouds join and grow heavy, they fall as rain, snow, sleet, or hail back toward Earth.

storyOfProcess.name: Collection and Runoff
storyOfProcess.description: Fallen water collects in rivers, lakes, soil, and oceans; some soaks into the ground and some flows back to larger bodies of water, ready to start the cycle again.

corePrinciples.name: Conservation of Mass
corePrinciples.description: Water is reused and reshaped but not created or destroyed, so the same water moves through the cycle over and over.

corePrinciples.name: Energy Drives Change
corePrinciples.description: Heat from the Sun causes evaporation and movement of water, so energy input controls how fast parts of the cycle happen.

corePrinciples.name: Phase Changes Matter
corePrinciples.description: Water changes between solid, liquid, and gas (ice, water, vapor); these phase changes cause clouds, precipitation, and storage in different places.
